label: Topic and angle
placeholder: Explain what the thread is about and the specific angle you want to take.
helper: Be specific enough to distinguish this thread from a general post about the topic.
why: The model cannot safely choose the user’s intended subject and point of view.

label: Primary goal
placeholder: Select the main outcome
helper: Choose the single most important result the thread should achieve.
why: The goal determines the thread’s progression, emphasis, ending, and CTA.

label: Target audience
placeholder: Who should read this, and what do they already know or care about?
helper: Describe the intended readers, their context, and their level of familiarity.
why: Audience knowledge and motivation materially affect terminology, examples, and depth.

label: Key points and facts
placeholder: List the facts, arguments, steps, examples, or details the thread must use.
helper: Include only information you want treated as factual or essential.
why: The thread requires user-supplied substance and must not invent supporting facts.

label: Output language
placeholder: For example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish
helper: You may also specify a regional variant, such as British English.
why: Language cannot always be inferred from the topic or source material.

label: Source material
placeholder: Paste notes, research, quotes, links, product details, or an existing draft.
helper: The model may reorganize supplied material but must not invent missing evidence.
why: Source material improves accuracy when the thread depends on research, quotations, or product information.

label: Thread type
placeholder: Select a thread format
helper: Choose the format closest to the way the idea should unfold.
why: Different X threads require different narrative structures rather than a generic hook-body-CTA formula.

label: Thread length
placeholder: Select a target length
helper: The final count may be reduced when the idea does not justify the selected length.
why: The desired thread length affects pacing and the amount of detail allocated to each point.

label: Main takeaway
placeholder: What should readers remember after finishing the thread?
helper: Leave blank when the takeaway can be inferred from the key points.
why: A supplied takeaway helps prevent the thread from becoming a disconnected list of posts.

label: Opening style
placeholder: Choose how the first post opens
helper: Auto selects the strongest truthful opening for the topic and goal.
why: The opening approach changes how the thread earns attention without relying on clickbait.

label: Level of detail
placeholder: Select the desired depth
helper: This controls information density, not the number of posts by itself.
why: Users may need either a fast overview or a more substantial explanation within the same post count.

label: Writing perspective
placeholder: Select who appears to be speaking
helper: Do not use first-person experience unless it is supported by the supplied information.
why: Perspective determines whether the thread reads as personal experience, brand communication, or independent explanation.

label: Post numbering
placeholder: Choose how posts are numbered
helper: Numbering is included inside the character allowance when used.
why: Numbering affects readability, character usage, and the appearance of the published thread.

label: Ending style
placeholder: Choose how the thread finishes
helper: The ending should resolve the thread rather than abruptly adding a sales pitch.
why: The intended ending changes how the final posts consolidate or convert reader attention.

label: Tone
placeholder: Select the overall tone
helper: The tone will remain appropriate to the subject and audience.
why: Tone is a genuine brand and communication preference that cannot always be inferred.

label: Voice guidance
placeholder: Describe your usual voice or paste a short example of how you write.
helper: Mention distinctive preferences, not general rules such as “write naturally.”
why: Specific voice guidance helps the output sound consistent with the user or brand.

label: Desired reader action
placeholder: Select the main action
helper: Auto chooses a proportionate CTA or omits it when none is useful.
why: The desired action determines whether and how the final post should convert attention.

label: CTA details
placeholder: Describe the action, offer, resource, or next step readers should take.
helper: Include only one primary next step and any information needed to explain it.
why: The model cannot safely invent an offer, resource, booking step, or conversion promise.

label: Destination URL
placeholder: https://example.com/resource
helper: Only supplied URLs may appear in the generated thread.
why: A link-based CTA requires an accurate user-supplied destination.
display: Show when `ctaMode` is `Visit a link`, `Subscribe`, `Download`, or `Buy or book`

label: Commercial relationship
placeholder: Select any relevant relationship
helper: This helps the thread avoid presenting promotion as independent opinion.
why: Commercial affiliation may materially change wording and disclosure requirements.

label: Disclosure wording
placeholder: For example: I built this product, or Sponsored by…
helper: Leave blank to generate a brief neutral disclosure based on the selected relationship.
why: The user may need approved or legally required disclosure wording.

label: Sensitive topic
placeholder: Select when additional caution is needed
helper: This enables cautious wording and verification notes where appropriate.
why: High-stakes subjects require different claim handling and may depend on jurisdiction or current authoritative information.

label: Claims and restrictions
placeholder: List prohibited claims, required wording, jurisdiction, confidentiality limits, or topics to avoid.
helper: Use this for genuine legal, factual, brand, privacy, or editorial restrictions.
why: The model cannot infer private restrictions, approved claims, or applicable jurisdiction.

label: Additional context
placeholder: Add any final context that materially affects the thread.
helper: Do not repeat information already entered in another field.
why: One controlled catch-all field supports uncommon but material requirements without expanding the form.
